title: Breaking: Scientists discover new Earth-like planet.
text: Astronomers from NASA's Kepler mission have identified a new planet that could potentially harbor life. The planet is in the habitable zone of its star.
label: REAL

title: New miracle cure for all diseases discovered in kitchen.
text: A secret ingredient in common black pepper can cure cancer, diabetes, and heart disease in just 24 hours, claims local blogger.
label: FAKE

title: The Federal Reserve announces interest rate hike.
text: The central bank of the United States has raised interest rates by 25 basis points to combat inflation and maintain economic stability.
label: REAL

title: NASA confirms Moon is actually made of cheese.
text: In a shocking announcement, NASA scientists revealed that the Moon is not rock, but a high-density lunar Swiss cheese.
label: FAKE

title: Tech giant releases new smartphone with AI integration.
text: A leading technology company has unveiled its latest smartphone, featuring deep integration with artificial intelligence for better user experience.
label: REAL

title: Invisible aliens have already invaded Earth, says expert.
text: A self-proclaimed UFO expert says that invisible aliens have been living among humans for decades and are controlling our thoughts.
label: FAKE

title: Local library starts new program for students.
text: The community library has launched a summer reading challenge to encourage literacy among local elementary and middle school students.
label: REAL

title: Winning lottery numbers can be predicted by cats.
text: New research suggests that house cats can accurately predict the next day's winning lottery numbers by the way they purr.
label: FAKE

title: Global stock markets show signs of recovery.
text: After a period of volatility, major global stock indices showed positive growth today as investor confidence returned.
label: REAL

title: Secret underwater city found in the middle of the Pacific.
text: Explorers have discovered a massive city built by an ancient civilization at the bottom of the Pacific Ocean, fully powered by glowing algae.
label: FAKE

title: World Health Organization declares end to global health emergency.
text: The WHO has officially announced that the recent global health crisis has subsided to a level where it no longer constitutes a public health emergency of international concern.
label: REAL

title: Drinking bleach cures viral infections, says social media post.
text: A viral post on social media platforms claims that consuming small amounts of bleach can effectively kill any virus in the human body. This is extremely dangerous.
label: FAKE

title: Electric vehicle sales reach record highs in Europe.
text: Recent data shows that electric vehicles now account for over 25% of all new car sales in major European markets, marking a significant shift in the automotive industry.
label: REAL

title: Government to implement 'thought taxes' by 2027.
text: Anonymous sources claim the government is developing technology to monitor citizen thoughts and will begin taxing 'unproductive' thinking by next year.
label: FAKE

title: Archaeologists find ancient scroll in Egypt.
text: A team of international archaeologists has unearthed a well-preserved papyrus scroll in Luxor, dating back to the New Kingdom period.
label: REAL

title: Clouds are actually giant surveillance drones.
text: A new conspiracy theory suggests that what we perceive as clouds are actually soft-bodied surveillance drones deployed by a secret global organization.
label: FAKE

title: Major breakthrough in fusion energy research.
text: Scientists at a national laboratory have achieved a net energy gain in a fusion reaction for the second time, bringing the world closer to clean, limitless energy.
label: REAL

title: WiFi signals are turning people into statues.
text: A blog post claims that long-term exposure to high-frequency WiFi signals causes a rare condition where human skin slowly turns into stone.
label: FAKE

title: New study links Mediterranean diet to longer life.
text: Researchers have published a decade-long study involving thousands of participants, confirming that a Mediterranean-style diet significantly reduces the risk of heart disease.
label: REAL

title: Time traveler from 2085 warns of chocolate shortage.
text: An individual claiming to be from the future says that by the year 2085, cacao trees will be extinct, and chocolate will be more valuable than gold.
label: FAKE

title: Olympic Games to include e-sports in upcoming cycle.
text: The International Olympic Committee has announced that several popular e-sports titles will be featured as medal events in the next summer games.
label: REAL

title: Trees communicate through secret underground internet.
text: While it sounds like fiction, scientists have discovered that trees use a network of fungi to share nutrients and warn each other of impending insect attacks.
label: REAL

title: Eating magnetic sand improves your internal GPS.
text: A lifestyle influencer claims that swallowing 'magnetized sand' helps humans develop a better sense of direction by aligning with the Earth's magnetic field.
label: FAKE

title: Mars rover finds evidence of ancient riverbeds.
text: The latest images and soil samples from the Mars rover 'Perseverance' strongly suggest that a massive river system once flowed through the Jezero Crater.
label: REAL

title: Water on Earth was brought here by giant space whales.
text: A fringe theory suggests that Earth's oceans did not come from comets, but were transported across the galaxy in the bellies of enormous biological space vessels.
label: FAKE

title: United Nations passes resolution on climate action.
text: Member states of the UN have reached a historic agreement to accelerate the transition to renewable energy and provide financial aid to developing nations for climate adaptation.
label: REAL

title: Using your phone in the dark makes your eyes glow.
text: A rumor spreading online suggests that the blue light from smartphones, when used in total darkness, causes a permanent bioluminescent effect in human retinas.
label: FAKE

title: Cancer survival rates have doubled in the last 40 years.
text: According to the National Cancer Institute, advancements in early detection and targeted therapies have led to a significant increase in survival rates for many types of cancer.
label: REAL

title: Gravity is slowing down, making us heavier.
text: A viral video claims that the Earth's gravitational pull is increasing every year due to 'space dust' accumulation, which is why people feel more tired lately.
label: FAKE

title: Artificial Intelligence helps doctors diagnose rare diseases.
text: New AI-powered tools are being used in hospitals to analyze complex medical images and patient data, identifying rare conditions that are often missed by human eyes.
label: REAL

title: The Sun is actually a giant lightbulb.
text: A conspiracy group claims that the Sun is an artificial construct maintained by an advanced civilization and that 'solar flares' are just the bulb flickering.
label: FAKE

title: Major city implements ban on single-use plastics.
text: The city council has voted to phase out plastic straws, bags, and containers in all restaurants and retail stores starting next month to reduce ocean pollution.
label: REAL

title: Birds aren't real, they are government spy cameras.
text: The 'Birds Aren't Real' movement continues to gain traction, claiming that the government replaced all birds with drones in the 1970s to monitor citizens.
label: FAKE

title: New vaccine shows 95% efficacy against malaria.
text: A groundbreaking clinical trial for a new malaria vaccine has shown highly promising results, potentially saving hundreds of thousands of lives annually in tropical regions.
label: REAL

title: Charging your phone in the microwave takes 30 seconds.
text: A dangerous 'life hack' circulating on social media claims that you can fully charge a smartphone battery by microwaving it for 30 seconds. Do not try this.
label: FAKE

title: SpaceX successfully launches mission to the asteroid belt.
text: The private space exploration company has successfully deployed a fleet of small probes designed to prospect for rare minerals on nearby asteroids.
label: REAL

title: Dinosaurs are still alive and living in the center of the Earth.
text: A popular 'Hollow Earth' theory suggests that dinosaurs did not go extinct but migrated to a tropical paradise located within the Earth's core.
label: FAKE

title: Global literacy rates reach an all-time high.
text: UNESCO reports that global literacy rates have increased to over 86% as a result of worldwide educational initiatives and digital access in remote areas.
label: REAL

title: The Great Wall of China was built to keep out dragons.
text: A viral 'history' blog claims that the Great Wall was not built for defense against nomadic tribes, but as a barrier against fire-breathing mythical creatures.
label: FAKE

title: Scientists develop biodegradable alternative to plastic.
text: A team of researchers has created a new material from seaweed that is fully compostable and can replace traditional plastic packaging within five years.
label: REAL

title: Laptops can be powered by your own body heat.
text: A tech startup claims to have invented a laptop that requires no battery, instead drawing power through the keyboard from the user's natural body temperature.
label: FAKE

title: Renewable energy capacity grew by 50% last year.
text: The International Energy Agency (IEA) reports a massive surge in wind and solar power installations, marking the fastest growth in two decades.
label: REAL

title: Sharks can smell fear from three miles away.
text: While sharks have a keen sense of smell, the idea that they can specifically detect the 'scent of fear' in humans from miles away is a myth.
label: FAKE

title: New species of deep-sea jellyfish discovered.
text: Marine biologists using a remote-operated vehicle in the Mariana Trench have filmed a previously unknown species of jellyfish with glowing red tentacles.
label: REAL

title: Cell towers are stealing your memories.
text: A fringe group claims that 5G cell towers emit a frequency that slowly erases short-term memories to make the population easier to control.
label: FAKE

title: Major bank introduces palm-print payments.
text: One of the world's largest banks has started rolling out biometric payment systems that allow customers to pay for groceries by simply scanning their palm.
label: REAL

title: Mountains are actually giant sleeping giants.
text: A 'geological' theory suggests that mountain ranges are not formed by tectonic plates, but are the fossilized remains of ancient titans that fell asleep.
label: FAKE

title: Global effort to plant one trillion trees by 2030.
text: A coalition of governments and NGOs has launched an ambitious project to restore forests worldwide as a primary strategy to capture carbon dioxide.
label: REAL

title: Coffee makes you shorter, says new report.
text: A fake health report suggests that drinking more than two cups of coffee a day inhibits bone growth and can make an adult lose up to two inches in height.
label: FAKE

title: Autonomous buses begin regular service in Tokyo.
text: The city has launched its first fleet of self-driving buses on public roads, serving a 5-mile loop in the business district with human safety monitors on board.
label: REAL

title: The Eiffel Tower grows six inches every summer.
text: Due to the thermal expansion of iron, the Eiffel Tower actually increases in height by about 15 centimeters during the hot summer months.
label: REAL

title: Wearing socks while sleeping causes permanent hair loss.
text: An unverified health blog claims that the heat trapped by socks during sleep confuses the body's 'hair growth signals,' leading to baldness over time.
label: FAKE

title: World's oldest person celebrates 120th birthday.
text: A woman in Japan, verified by Guinness World Records, has celebrated her 120th birthday, crediting her longevity to a diet of fish and green tea.
label: REAL

title: Internet will be shut down for 'maintenance' for 48 hours.
text: A viral message warns that the entire global internet will be turned off this weekend for a worldwide software update. This is a hoax.
label: FAKE

title: Researchers create 'unbreakable' glass for smartphones.
text: Using a new crystalline structure, material scientists have developed a glass that is ten times more resistant to impact than current leading products.
label: REAL

title: Gold can be made from lead using a kitchen blender.
text: A 'chemistry hack' video claims that if you blend lead pellets with a 'secret' vinegar solution, they will eventually turn into 24-karat gold.
label: FAKE

title: New coral reef found in unexpected location.
text: Scientists have discovered a massive, healthy coral reef system in the dark, cold waters of the northern Atlantic, challenging previous assumptions about coral habitats.
label: REAL

title: Rainwater is actually government tears.
text: A satirical but widely shared post claims that rain is not part of the water cycle, but the result of government officials crying over tax revenue.
label: FAKE

title: Major breakthrough in Alzheimer's treatment.
text: A new drug that clears protein plaques in the brain has shown significant success in slowing cognitive decline in early-stage Alzheimer's patients.
label: REAL

title: Your shadow can move independently if you don't watch it.
text: A 'paranormal' website claims that human shadows are sentient beings that only mimic our movements because they are shy, but move freely when unobserved.
label: FAKE

title: Electric planes take flight for short-haul routes.
text: A regional airline has successfully completed its first commercial-style test flight of a fully electric 19-seater aircraft, aiming for regular service by 2026.
label: REAL

title: Sleeping with a fan on 'drinks' your oxygen.
text: A common myth in some regions suggests that running a fan in a closed room while you sleep will consume all the oxygen and lead to suffocation.
label: FAKE

title: Hubble telescope captures image of the 'Pillars of Creation'.
text: NASA has released a stunning new high-resolution image of the famous gas clouds in the Eagle Nebula, showing intricate details of star formation.
label: REAL

title: The moon is a hologram projected from Antarctica.
text: Flat-earth theorists claim that the moon is not a physical object but a light projection intended to hide the truth about the Earth's shape.
label: FAKE

title: Global population reaches 8 billion milestone.
text: The United Nations has officially announced that the world's population has surpassed 8 billion people, highlighting challenges for sustainable development.
label: REAL

title: Carrots were originally purple before being genetically modified.
text: Historically, most cultivated carrots were indeed purple, yellow, or white; the orange variety was developed in the Netherlands in the 17th century.
label: REAL

title: Drinking cold water freezes your internal organs.
text: A viral health warning claims that drinking ice water after a meal causes your stomach and intestines to instantly freeze, leading to digestive failure.
label: FAKE

title: Ancient city of Pompeii reveals new secrets.
text: Ongoing excavations at the site of the Vesuvius eruption have uncovered a perfectly preserved 'snack bar' with remains of food still in the jars.
label: REAL

title: Mars has a secret atmosphere made of breathable oxygen.
text: A conspiracy theorist claims that NASA is lying about Mars' atmosphere and that astronauts are already living there without spacesuits.
label: FAKE

title: New technology allows paralyzed man to walk using thoughts.
text: Using a 'digital bridge' between the brain and spinal cord, scientists have enabled a man with a severed spine to regain control over his leg muscles.
label: REAL

title: Touching a toad will give you warts.
text: While toads have bumpy skin, the idea that they can transmit warts to humans is a myth; warts are caused by human viruses, not amphibians.
label: FAKE

title: Global shipping rates drop as supply chains stabilize.
text: After years of disruption, the cost of moving containers across the oceans has returned to pre-pandemic levels, easing inflationary pressures.
label: REAL

title: If you sneeze with your eyes open, they will pop out.
text: Despite common fears, the muscles and tissues holding your eyes in place are far too strong for a sneeze to dislodge them.
label: FAKE

title: Discovery of a new species of 'glass' frog.
text: Researchers in the Amazon rainforest have identified a tiny frog with completely transparent skin on its underbelly, revealing its beating heart.
label: REAL

title: WiFi was invented by aliens in 1947.
text: A 'hidden history' book claims that WiFi technology was recovered from a crashed UFO in Roswell and kept secret for decades.
label: FAKE

title: First successful 3D-printed heart transplant in a pig.
text: Medical researchers have successfully transplanted a 3D-printed organ made from biological tissue into a pig, a major step toward human applications.
label: REAL

title: Bananas are radioactive and can kill you.
text: While bananas contain small amounts of radioactive potassium-40, you would need to eat roughly 10 million bananas at once to die of radiation poisoning.
label: FAKE

title: Rare 'Blue Moon' to appear this Friday.
text: Astronomers are encouraging people to look up this weekend as a seasonal Blue Moon—the third of four full moons in a single season—will be visible.
label: REAL

title: The Earth's core is actually made of cotton candy.
text: A satirical science site claims that the 'molten iron' core is a lie and that the Earth is actually filled with high-pressure pink spun sugar.
label: FAKE

title: Major tech company announces 'Carbon Negative' goal.
text: A leading software firm has pledged to remove more carbon from the atmosphere than it emits by 2030, including historical emissions since its founding.
label: REAL

title: Culls of 'ugly' animals planned to improve nature's aesthetics.
text: A fake news report claims that a new environmental agency wants to remove 'unattractive' species to make national parks more 'Instagrammable.'
label: FAKE

title: Scientists find water ice on the surface of the Moon.
text: Data from a lunar orbiter has confirmed the presence of water ice in the permanently shadowed craters at the Moon's poles, a key resource for future bases.
label: REAL

title: Swallowing gum takes seven years to digest.
text: While the body cannot digest the synthetic base of chewing gum, it does not stay in your stomach; it passes through your system just like anything else.
label: FAKE

title: New algorithm can predict volcanic eruptions weeks in advance.
text: Using machine learning to analyze seismic patterns and gas emissions, researchers have developed a model that significantly improves eruption warnings.
label: REAL

title: The Great Pyramid was a giant wireless power plant.
text: A popular pseudoscientific theory suggests that the pyramids of Giza were built to transmit electricity globally through the ionosphere.
label: FAKE

title: Historic peace treaty signed between two warring nations.
text: After decades of conflict, leaders of the two countries have signed a comprehensive peace agreement, opening borders and restoring diplomatic ties.
label: REAL

title: Cracking your knuckles causes arthritis.
text: Numerous studies have shown that the sound of knuckle cracking is just gas bubbles bursting and does not lead to arthritis or joint damage.
label: REAL

title: Record-breaking heatwave hits Australia.
text: Meteorologists report that several cities have seen temperatures exceeding 45 degrees Celsius, leading to emergency warnings and power grid strain.
label: REAL

title: Looking at a picture of a fire can burn your house down.
text: A superstitious blog post claims that keeping digital images of fire on your computer creates a 'quantum link' that can spontaneously ignite furniture.
label: FAKE

title: Scientists create the first 'synthetic' human embryo.
text: Using stem cells and no egg or sperm, researchers have created embryo-like structures that mimic the early stages of human development for research.
label: REAL

title: Elephants can fly if they believe in themselves.
text: A fake 'nature documentary' clip being shared online claims that African elephants have the ability to glide using their ears, but only when happy.
label: FAKE

title: New solar panels work even at night.
text: Engineers have developed a 'thermoradiative cell' that generates a small amount of electricity by capturing the heat radiating from the Earth toward space at night.
label: REAL

title: If you stare at a microwave, it will read your mind.
text: A conspiracy website claims that the radiation from microwave ovens can 'ping' human brainwaves and send your private thoughts back to the manufacturer.
label: FAKE

title: Breakthrough in battery technology uses common salt.
text: A new type of sodium-ion battery has entered mass production, offering a cheaper and more sustainable alternative to lithium-ion for energy storage.
label: REAL

title: The sky is blue because we live inside a giant blue eye.
text: A 'spiritual' theory suggests that our universe is located inside the eye of a colossal being and the sky's color is just its iris.
label: FAKE

title: World Bank predicts global economic growth of 3% this year.
text: In its latest report, the World Bank suggests that despite various challenges, the global economy is showing resilience and steady recovery.
label: REAL

title: Vultures are actually highly efficient recyclers of nature.
text: Unlike their negative reputation, vultures play a critical role in ecosystems by removing carcasses that would otherwise spread diseases to other animals.
label: REAL

title: A secret button on every ATM gives you double cash.
text: A viral 'finance tip' claims that if you press 'Cancel' and '7' at the same time, the ATM will double your withdrawal for free. This is false.
label: FAKE

title: First commercial space hotel planned for 2027.
text: A space construction company has unveiled plans for a rotating orbital station that will include luxury accommodations and research facilities for paying guests.
label: REAL

title: Drinking the juice of a cactus makes you invisible to spiders.
text: A fake survival guide claims that the chemical composition of certain cacti, when ingested, masks the human scent from all arachnids.
label: FAKE
